metaDescription: Prosperity is a Minecraft 1.21.1 Fabric mod that gives every player their own instanced loot from naturally generated containers, with distance-scaled quality, unlooted indicators, and a loot modifier API.
ogDescription: An instanced loot overhaul for Minecraft 1.21.1 Fabric. Per-player loot, unlooted indicators, distance-scaled quality, and a loot modifier API.

hero.sub: Prosperity gives every player their own instanced loot from naturally generated containers — no more racing to chests on shared servers — and rewards venturing farther from spawn with better loot.

sections.title: Core Systems
sections.lede: Four systems that make loot fair on shared worlds and worth the journey. Full feature list →

items.title: Instanced Loot
html: Every player gets their own loot from each naturally generated container — chests, barrels, and more. A drop-in replacement for Lootr.

items.title: Unlooted Indicators
html: A gold sparkle hovers above containers you haven't opened yet. No more backtracking through strongholds and mansions.

items.title: Distance Scaling
html: The farther a container sits from world origin, the better its loot — five tiers from Local through Depths, with bigger stacks and rarer items.

items.title: Loot Modifier API
html: A stable, Fabric-style event lets other mods adjust loot generation, plus datapack-driven injection by distance tier.

sections.title: Five Distance Tiers
sections.lede: Loot quality scales with absolute distance from world origin. Each tier raises stack quantities and the quality (luck) of what spawns.
sections.footnote: Tiers are configurable, and structures can override the tier they use. See the breakdown →

items.title: Local & Frontier
html: 0–999 blocks is baseline vanilla loot. From 1,000 blocks, Frontier tier delivers 1.5× stacks and a +1 quality boost.

items.title: Wilderness & Outlands
html: 3,000+ blocks doubles stacks at Wilderness; 6,000+ reaches Outlands at 2.75× — worth the long expedition.

items.title: Depths
html: Beyond 10,000 blocks, Depths tier delivers the best possible loot at 3.5× stacks and +4 quality. The End is always treated as Depths.

sections.title: Quick Install
sections.lede: Requires Fabric Loader 0.16.10+, Fabric API, and Java 21 on Minecraft 1.21.1.

items: Install Fabric Loader 0.16.10+ for Minecraft 1.21.1.
Drop prosperity-x.x.x.jar into your mods/ folder (server and client).
Launch the game. Config generates at config/prosperity.json on first run.
Adjust settings in the config file or hot-reload with /prosperity reload in-game.
blocks.note: Migrating from Lootr? Remove it first. New to the mod? Start with the Getting Started guide.
